Primary arthrodesis (1st-3rd TMT): for purely ligamentous, delayed, chronic, or complete dislocations (Type A/C2). Denude cartilage, screws/plates +/- graft. Equivalent or superior

must_know_before_case: Perthes: idiopathic AVN of proximal femoral epiphysis in children. Lateral pillar classification prognostic. Containment limits deformity.
Proximal femoral varus osteotomy or pelvic (Salter/Dega/shelf) for containment in >8yo or lateral pillar B/B-C. Anterior or lateral approaches.

must_know_before_case: Lateral ankle: ATFL, CFL, PTFL. ATFL most common injured. Brostrom anatomic repair or with Gould augmentation.
Sural nerve / peroneal tendons
Lateral malleolus, sinus tarsi, peroneal tubercle
Anterior or curvilinear lateral incision over ATFL/CFL. Imbricate or reattach to fibula with anchors. Gould augmentation (inferior extensor retinaculum) for augmentation.

must_know_before_case: Metacarpals: 2nd-5th most common. Boxer's (5th neck) common. Apex dorsal angulation deforming force from interossei.
Extensor tendons / sagittal bands
Dorsal approach for ORIF (plate or K-wire). For boxer's 5th, often closed reduction percutaneous or open if rotated/shortened. Acceptable angulation varies by digit (more in 5th).

must_know_before_case: Distal femur: medial/lateral condyles, trochlea, intercondylar notch. Hoffa (coronal) fragment variant. Gastroc deforming force.
Popliteal vessels / peroneal n.
Parapatellar (medial or lateral) or combined for intra-articular. Lateral for extra-articular. Protect extensor mechanism and posterior structures.

must_know_before_case: Lateral epicondyle: ECRB origin primary pathology (angiofibroblastic hyperplasia). Kocher interval between anconeus (radial n.) and ECU (PIN).
Lateral collateral ligament / PIN
Lateral (Kocher) approach: release/debride ECRB origin, repair or leave. Avoid LCL injury. Arthroscopic option for some.
